steps.title: Identify the contrast structure
steps.content: The sentence reads: "its role isn't ______; rather, the accessory spleen may actively support diving mechanisms." The word "rather" signals a direct contrast. The blank must be the opposite of "actively supporting" a function — something meaning dormant, hidden, or without active role.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'replicable': means capable of being reproduced. The text addresses whether the organ has a function, not whether that function can be replicated. Irrelevant meaning rule violated. ✗
  • Choice B — 'predetermined': means decided in advance. The text focuses on whether the organ is active or dormant, not on whether its role was determined beforehand. Irrelevant meaning rule violated. ✗
  • Choice C — 'operative': means functional or active. Saying the role "isn't operative" would mean it isn't functional — but "rather, it actively supports diving" also says it is functional, creating a contradiction rather than a contrast. Logical contradiction rule violated. ✗
  • Choice D — 'latent': means dormant or hidden, not actively expressed. Saying the role "isn't latent" contrasts cleanly with "actively supports diving mechanisms." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
